content.text: I need a method to remove an item from my internal dictionary based on its zero-based position in an external list.

This function works by first looking up the identifier (the 'key') of the item currently at the specified index using a helper object. Once retrieved, it uses standard Python del syntax to remove that key from your internal items tracking.

```python
def delete(self, a_position):
        "Deletes the item at the zero-based index 'n' from the control."
        key = self.wx_obj.GetPyData(a_position)
        del self._items[key]
```

The implementation is concise, assuming wx_obj reliably maps indices to keys. If the provided position is invalid or the resulting key isn't in _items, it will raise an IndexError or KeyError.
What kind of error happens if I try to delete a position that doesn't actually exist?

If `a_position` exceeds the bounds accessible by `self.wx_obj`, it might raise an index related exception from there. If the key is valid but not in `self._items`, Python will raise a KeyError when executing the del statement.
Got it, so I need to handle those potential errors on my end.
